title: decomposition orchestrator turn output
description: Structured-output generation schema for ONE orchestrator decision turn's full output envelope. The orchestrator emits { control_action, produced_content? }: control_action is the exactly-one control action that drives the run (the same shape as orchestrator-control-action.schema.json), and produced_content is a SIBLING object carrying the turn's authored draft (NOT a control-action field, so the control envelope stays minimal — terminate remains exactly {action,outcome,reason}). The harness validates ONLY the control envelope (orchestrator-control-action.mjs parseControlAction is the contract of record); produced_content is the orchestrator's free authored output, gated only at the single commit. On terminate with outcome commit, produced_content MUST carry final_issues + project_update_markdown; this schema keeps those optional at generation time so non-terminating turns and pause/failed_closed terminals validate.

control_action.description: The exactly-one control action for this turn. invoke_library (run a named library subagent), invoke_one_off (run an improvised inline subagent), or terminate (end the run). parseControlAction enforces the per-action field + enum rules (the one-off runtime_role whitelist and the terminate outcome/reason pairs); terminate is exactly {action,outcome,reason} with no extra fields.

target_key.description: invoke_library only: the accepted-prompt manifest target_key of the library subagent to run.

instance_id.description: invoke_library/invoke_one_off optional: subagent instance address. Omit for the role's default instance; supply a fresh safe id to spawn a non-default same-role instance; reuse a prior resolved instance_id to continue it.

role_label.description: invoke_one_off only: a short human label for the improvised subagent's perspective.

task.description: invoke_one_off only: what the one-off subagent should do.

prompt.description: invoke_one_off only: the full system prompt body for the improvised subagent.

runtime_role.description: invoke_one_off only: which invocable runtime role the one-off runs on. The orchestrator's own 'orchestrator' role is the driver and is never a one-off target.

outcome.description: terminate only: the terminal outcome the orchestrator chose. failed_closed is NOT here — the harness emits it on a bounds/environment breach.

reason.description: terminate only: the terminal reason (commit -> synthesis_complete; pause -> product_questions | discovery_needed | needs_pm_review).

produced_content.description: The turn's authored draft — a SIBLING of control_action, never a control-action field. On terminate(commit) this MUST include final_issues + project_update_markdown (the run_id line + the '## What I did with each part of your project' section). Every field is optional at generation time so non-terminating turns and pause/failed_closed terminals validate; the single commit floor (validateOrchestratorOutput) is the real enforcement.

final_issues.description: terminate(commit) only: the agent-ready issue set. Each issue carries decomposition_key, title, issue_body_markdown, depends_on, assignment, output, acceptance_criteria, plus optional work_type and resource_target.

decomposition_key.description: Stable lower_snake/kebab key unique within the issue set; depends_on entries reference these keys.

resource_target.description: Optional AF-owned execution resource selector for this issue.

project_update_markdown.description: The project-level update posted on commit/pause/failed_closed. On commit it must include the run_id line and the '## What I did with each part of your project' accountability section.

context_digest.description: A short prose digest of the project intent + constraints the orchestrator grounded the decomposition on.

source_refs.description: The references the decomposition drew on (e.g. { kind: 'linear_project', id }}).

open_questions_markdown.description: pause/failed_closed only: the open questions the human should resolve before decomposition continues.

discovery_issues.description: pause(discovery_needed) only: improvised discovery issues to create instead of a full agent-ready set.
